ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic toast class (FOODON_00002683); no specific multigrain butter thick toast entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no specific multigrain thick toast with butter code exists. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559003 is a generic toast concept; no multigrain butter thick toast specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two thick slices of multigrain toast spread with butter, weighing approximately 120 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, whole-wheat, commercially prepared (USDA SR Legacy #18069) adapted with butter addition; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo thick toast entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Multigrain thick toast with butter is not listed as a single entry in any approved primary source. Values are derived by adapting USDA SR Legacy whole-wheat bread (FDC ID 325871) as the multigrain bread base and adding a standard butter spread contribution (~10 g butter per 100 g composite), consistent with HPB Singapore Foodo thick toast with butter reference ranges. Energy, fat, and saturated fat are adjusted upward to reflect the butter component. All values represent the composite food as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of whole-grain and multigrain breads — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Toast Box multigrain thick toast with butter. GI of 65 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables, which report whole-grain and multigrain bread GI values in the range 53–74 (mean ~65). The butter spread has minimal effect on GI. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 53.4) / 100 ≈ 34.7, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 44.5 × 1.2 = 53.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~456 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this toast as the sodium content from bread and butter combined (~456 mg per serving) contributes approximately 20% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; choosing a low-sodium bread variant or reducing butter quantity can help manage intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 65) and high glycemic load per serving (~34.7)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this toast in moderation because its medium GI (65) and high glycemic load per serving (~34.7) can cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with a protein source such as eggs or limiting portion to one slice may help attenuate the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter (~5.2 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the saturated fat contribution from butter (~6.2 g per serving) and moderate sodium load warrant portion control; the multigrain bread base provides dietary fibre (~5 g per serving) which may support LDL cholesterol management, but butter quantity should be minimised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Butter Thick Toast is a popular Singaporean kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) breakfast item consisting of two thick slices of multigrain bread that are toasted until golden and crisp on the outside while remaining soft inside, then generously spread with salted butter. The multigrain bread base typically incorporates a blend of wheat, oats, seeds, and other whole grains, giving the toast a slightly nutty flavour, a denser crumb, and a higher dietary fibre content compared to white bread toast. The butter spread adds richness, a creamy mouthfeel, and contributes to the food's fat and saturated fat profile. This dish is a signature menu item at Toast Box, a well-known Singaporean café chain, and is commonly served alongside a soft-boiled egg and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea) as part of a traditional Singaporean breakfast set. Nutritionally, the food provides moderate energy (~298 kcal per 100 g), meaningful dietary fibre (~4.2 g per 100 g), and B-vitamins from the multigrain base, but carries a moderate glycemic index (GI ~65) and a notable sodium load from both the bread and butter. Clinically, it is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its medium-to-high glycemic load per serving, and for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source entry exists for multigrain butter thick toast as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy whole-wheat bread data with a calculated butter contribution, cross-referenced against HPB Singapore Foodo thick toast reference ranges. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous multigrain bread entries. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is methodologically sound but not an exact match to a primary source entry.
notes: Vitamin C is 0.0 as multigrain bread with butter contains no meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for whole-wheat bread. All -1 values for micronutrients (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g) are reported as numeric estimates from USDA SR Legacy and are not -1 in this record. Potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water are estimated from USDA SR Legacy whole-wheat bread (FDC 325871) adjusted for butter addition; these are best-estimate values and should be verified against an HPB Singapore Foodo direct entry if one becomes available. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database multigrain bread entries (Atkinson et al. 2021). Glycemic load per serving calculated at 120 g serving size: GL = (65 × 53.4) / 100 ≈ 34.7. Halal status assumed based on ingredients (no pork, no alcohol); formal halal certification status of Toast Box products should be verified with MUIS. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact multigrain butter thick toast code exists in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT.
